docs.title: Core Assembly
docs.text: View Materials List

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Core is the removable power module that stores and releases energy for firing. It is built in two parts:

  • Plunger Sub-Assembly
  • Final Assembly

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Confirm all sliding interfaces are low-friction before final fastening.
  • Incorrect orientation of the plunger assembly can jam the core.

docs.title: Step 1: Install catch on prime block
docs.text:
• Slide Catch onto the PrimeBlock assembly so it sits just in front of CatchLever.
  • Ensure orientation is correct: point side down, larger face toward PrimeBlock.

docs.title: Step 2: Install catch lever spring
docs.text:
• Rotate CatchLever down and insert a 0.625 inch compression spring in the PrimeBlock spring pocket.
  • Rotate CatchLever up to trap the spring in the lever indent.
  • Slide Catch under CatchLever to lock engagement.

docs.title: Step 3: Attach catch to pump bars
docs.text:
• Attach Catch to PumpBars with two CatchBearings and two 6-32 socket head screws.
  • Loctite is highly recommended.

docs.title: Step 4: Install plunger assembly into prime block
docs.text:
• Align CatchEnd angle with Catch face.
  • Slide Plunger Assembly into position.
  • Secure with two 6-32 socket head screws.
  • Verify orientation before fastening to avoid core jam.

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• PlungerHead
  • CatchTube
  • CatchEnd
  • SpringBase
  • Spring
  • Catch
  • CatchLever
  • CatchBearing x2

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text:
• 6-32 Heat Set Insert x4
  • 6-32 Socket Head Screw x8
  • 6-32 Phillips Screw x4
  • 0.625 inch Compression Spring x1
  • 127 O-Ring x2
  • 016 O-Ring x1

docs.title: Consumables
docs.text:
• Loctite (recommended)
  • Lubricant

docs.title: Variant Materials
docs.text: • Core spring and handedness variant selections are covered at the configuration stage.

docs.title: Step 1: Prep sliding interfaces
docs.text:
• Clean center through-holes of PlungerHead, CatchEnd, and SpringBase.
  • Remove stray plastic and smooth only as needed to reduce friction.
  • Keep key internal ridges intact.

docs.title: Step 2: Test-fit on barrel and catch tube
docs.text:
• Verify PlungerHead and CatchEnd slide freely on Barrel.
  • Verify SpringBase slides freely on CatchTube.
  • Adjust fit only until motion is smooth with minimal resistance.

docs.title: Step 3: Align catch tube in plunger head
docs.text:
• Insert CatchTube into PlungerHead and rotate until hole patterns align.
  • Confirm all four holes align, then rotate to hide holes for insert installation.

docs.title: Step 4: Install heat-set inserts and o-rings
docs.text:
• Install four 6-32 heat set inserts in PlungerHead until seated against CatchTube.
  • Let cool, verify CatchTube rotation, then remove CatchTube.
  • Install one 127 o-ring around PlungerHead and one 016 o-ring inside.
  • Install a second 127 o-ring as front bumper, or use optional TPU bumper.

docs.title: Step 5: Install catch end
docs.text:
• Slide CatchEnd onto CatchTube and align holes.
  • Install four 6-32 socket head screws and tighten flush without overtightening.
  • Loctite is recommended.
  • Confirm CatchEnd slides freely on Barrel.

docs.title: Step 6: Install spring base and spring
docs.text:
• Slide SpringBase onto CatchTube with angled face oriented toward CatchEnd.
  • Rotate until angled faces align.
  • Slide Spring onto CatchTube and seat spring base in the groove.

docs.title: Step 7: Install plunger head screws
docs.text:
• Push PlungerHead onto CatchTube fully and align holes.
  • Install four 6-32 Phillips screws into the aligned CatchTube holes.
  • Verify screw heads sit below the 127 o-ring to avoid tube scraping.
  • Confirm SpringBase can move freely and compress the spring.

docs.title: Step 8: Lubricate and motion check
docs.text:
• Apply lubricant to the inner 016 o-ring.
  • Verify full assembly moves freely on Barrel after lube distribution.

docs.title: Plunger 1
docs.text: Prep PlungerHead, CatchEnd, and SpringBase sliding interfaces.

docs.title: Plunger 2
docs.text: Install CatchTube, heat-set inserts, and o-rings.

docs.title: Plunger 3
docs.text: Install CatchEnd, SpringBase, Spring, and PlungerHead screws.

docs.title: Plunger 4
docs.text: Lubricate and verify free motion on barrel.

docs.title: Final 1
docs.text: Install Catch and CatchLever spring in PrimeBlock.

docs.title: Final 2
docs.text: Attach Catch to PumpBars with CatchBearings.

docs.title: Final 3
docs.text: Install Plunger Assembly into PrimeBlock with correct orientation.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text: This section captures supported variant combinations and style options.

docs.title: Current Coverage
docs.text:
• Shroud variants
  • Prime block action variants
  • Mid body options

docs.title: In Progress
docs.text: A full compatibility matrix and visual guide will be added in a later pass.

docs.title: Final Assembly
docs.text: View Materials List

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This section combines the completed sub-assemblies into a working blaster and performs final fit/tension checks.

docs.title: Step 1: Install body bars
docs.text:
• Insert both BodyBars into the StockAssembly with circular notch facing up.
  • Align side holes and lock with a quick-release pin.

docs.title: Step 2: Prepare transfer bar and turnaround
docs.text:
• Remove TransferBarAdapter from TransferBar.
  • Slide TransferBar through Turnaround rear cutout until Pusher seals in Turnaround.

docs.title: Step 3: Install turnaround assembly on body bars
docs.text:
• Slide Turnaround, PlungerTube, and TransferBar assembly onto BodyBars.
  • Align stock nubs with Turnaround cutouts and seat fully flush.

docs.title: Step 4: Install tube spacer and tube holder
docs.text:
• Install TubeSpacer with cutout facing rear toward Magwell.
  • Install TubeHolder with TubeHolderInsert facing away from PlungerTube.
  • Reattach TransferBarAdapter with cutout side down.

docs.title: Step 5: Install receiver assembly
docs.text: • Slide ReceiverAssembly onto TubeHolder until flush.

docs.title: Step 6: Lubricate core interfaces
docs.text: • Apply lubricant to outer 127 o-rings on PlungerHead, inner 016 o-ring, and inside CatchEnd.

docs.title: Step 7: Install core
docs.text:
• Slide Core into PlungerTube through Receiver.
  • Seat PrimeBlock so raised rear section clicks behind TransferBarAdapter front section.
  • Confirm PlungerHead travels smoothly in PlungerTube.

docs.title: Step 8: Install mid body
docs.text:
• Slide selected MidBody onto Receiver, aligning dowels, nubs, and cutouts.
  • Seat fully flush.

docs.title: Step 9: Install barrel
docs.text:
• Apply a light lube ring around Barrel about 1 inch from rear edge.
  • Insert Barrel through PrimeBlock until fully seated in Turnaround.

docs.title: Step 10: Install gear tensioner assembly
docs.text: • Slide GearTensionerAssembly onto front until flush with MidBody.

docs.title: Step 11: Pin and tension
docs.text:
• Rotate OuterGear to align TensionerBlock hole with BodyBars notch.
  • Insert quick-release pin.
  • Hand-tighten OuterGear until assembly is stiff and no further hand rotation is possible.

docs.title: Step 12: Lock barrel
docs.text:
• Ensure Barrel is fully seated in Turnaround.
  • Tighten Gear Tensioner thumbscrew to lock barrel in place.
  • Do not overtighten.

docs.title: Step 13: Optional SCAR install
docs.text:
• Install two 016 o-rings on SCAR rear grooves.
  • Press SCAR onto barrel front until it snaps into place.

docs.title: Step 14: Functional check
docs.text:
• Insert compatible magazine.
  • Prime until catch engages, then return handle forward to load.
  • In a safe direction, pull trigger to verify function.

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• BodyBars x2
  • TubeSpacer
  • ReceiverAssembly
  • CoreAssembly
  • MidBody (selected variant)
  • Barrel
  • GearTensionerAssembly

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text: • Quick-Release Pin x2

docs.title: Consumables
docs.text: • Lubricant

docs.title: SCAR
docs.text:
• SCAR x1
  • 016 O-Ring x2

docs.title: Step 1
docs.text: Install BodyBars and quick-release pin in StockAssembly.

docs.title: Step 2
docs.text: Assemble Turnaround, TransferBar, TubeSpacer, and TubeHolder stack.

docs.title: Step 3
docs.text: Install ReceiverAssembly and Core, then verify smooth core travel.

docs.title: Step 4
docs.text: Install MidBody and Barrel.

docs.title: Step 5
docs.text: Install GearTensionerAssembly, pin, tension, and lock barrel.

docs.title: Step 6
docs.text: Optional: install SCAR and run final function check.

docs.title: Gear Tensioner Assembly
docs.text: View Materials List

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Build the front connector and gear train, then install lock and tensioning hardware.

docs.title: Step 1: Prepare inner gear screw and nut
docs.text:
• Insert a 1-1/4 inch 10-32 screw into InnerGear from the circular side.
  • Thread a 10-32 nut until seated in the underside cutout.
  • Apply a small amount of superglue to secure the screw head/threads and seat fully.
  • Allow adhesive to cure before continuing.

docs.title: Step 2: Build connector cap gear stack
docs.text:
• Place FrontConnectorCap on the selected shroud.
  • Fasten with two 6-32 x 5/16 screws in the inner bottom holes.
  • Lightly lubricate the gear bed and install BarrelGear, SideGears with 3/4 inch dowels, OuterGear, and InnerGear.
  • Place washer above InnerGear.

docs.title: Step 3: Prepare tensioner block
docs.text:
• Insert a 10-32 nut into TensionerBlock.
  • Press NutCap in until it clicks into side grooves.
  • Test fit the tensioner pin path across the block.

docs.title: Step 4: Install heat set insert
docs.text:
• Install a 6-32 heat set insert into FrontConnectorBase top hole.
  • Press straight and flush using a soldering iron at correct temperature.

docs.title: Step 5: Join base to cap assembly
docs.text:
• Align both dowels, the center inner-gear screw hole, and top side grooves.
  • Press FrontConnectorBase onto FrontConnectorCap until snug.

docs.title: Step 6: Install lock hardware
docs.text:
• Fasten selected shroud into FrontConnectorBase with one 6-32 x 5/16 screw from top.
  • Install two 6-32 x 5/8 socket head bolts from shroud side.
  • Insert two 6-32 nuts into side slots.
  • Install two LockBlocks (tapered side down), then LockBlockCap and secure with one 6-32 x 5/16 screw.

docs.title: Step 7: Install tensioner block and thumbscrews
docs.text:
• Insert TensionerBlock from bottom with smaller face up.
  • Thread two 6-32 thumbscrews into captive nuts above LockBlocks.
  • Rotate OuterGear to move TensionerBlock until through-hole aligns.
  • Verify thumbscrews drive LockBlocks inward, then back off for later barrel install.

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• FrontConnectorCap
  • FrontConnectorBase
  • TensionerBlock
  • NutCap
  • LockBlock x2
  • LockBlockCap
  • BarrelGear
  • SideGear x2
  • OuterGear
  • InnerGear

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text:
• 3/32 inch Dowel, 3/4 inch x2
  • 10-32 Nut x2
  • 10-32 Oval Head Screw, 1-1/4 inch x1
  • 6-32 Heat Set Insert x1
  • Washer x1
  • 6-32 Nut x2
  • 6-32 Socket Head Bolt, 5/8 inch x2
  • 6-32 Phillips Screw, 5/16 inch x4
  • 6-32 Thumbscrew x2

docs.title: Consumables
docs.text:
• Superglue
  • Lubricant (light)

docs.title: Components
docs.text: • Railgun shroud or Bipod shroud (choose one)

docs.title: Step 1
docs.text: Prepare InnerGear with 10-32 screw and nut, then glue and cure.

docs.title: Step 2
docs.text: Build FrontConnectorCap gear stack and install washer.

docs.title: Step 3
docs.text: Install 10-32 nut and NutCap in TensionerBlock.

docs.title: Step 4
docs.text: Install 6-32 heat set insert in FrontConnectorBase.

docs.title: Step 5
docs.text: Align and press FrontConnectorBase onto FrontConnectorCap assembly.

docs.title: Step 6
docs.text: Install shroud screw, socket bolts, nuts, lock blocks, and LockBlockCap.

docs.title: Step 7
docs.text: Install TensionerBlock and thumbscrews, verify lock motion.

docs.title: Loader Assembly
docs.text: View Materials List

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Loader Assembly moves with the core to load a dart and maintain a seal into the turnaround/barrel interface.

docs.title: Step 1: Set pusher seal
docs.text:
• Install a 1/4-20 x 3/8 screw through a 202 o-ring into the front of the Pusher.
  • Tune tightness so the seal is airtight in the Turnaround while still allowing smooth priming.
  • Bolt action usually tolerates a looser seal; straight pull generally needs a tighter seal.

docs.title: Step 2: Fit transfer bar adapter
docs.text:
• Test-fit TransferBarAdapter onto the front of TransferBar.
  • Confirm it seats flush in the cutout and can be removed/reinstalled smoothly.
  • Sand or tolerance-tune as needed.

docs.title: Step 3: Attach pusher to transfer bar
docs.text:
• Install Pusher onto TransferBar with two 6-32 socket head screws.
  • Keep pusher on the same side as the TransferBarAdapter center cutout.

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• Pusher
  • TransferBar
  • TransferBarAdapter

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text:
• 1/4-20 Screw, 3/8 inch x1
  • 202 O-Ring x1
  • 6-32 Socket Head Screw x2

docs.title: Variant Materials
docs.text: • Seal tension can vary by action type and tuning preference.

docs.title: Step 1
docs.text: Install pusher seal screw and 202 o-ring, then tune seal drag.

docs.title: Step 2
docs.text: Fit TransferBarAdapter on TransferBar and adjust tolerances if needed.

docs.title: Step 3
docs.text: Install Pusher on TransferBar using two 6-32 socket head screws.

docs.title: Prime Block Assembly
docs.text: View Materials List

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Prime block assembly has one shared build path and multiple action variants.

docs.title: Build Paths
docs.text:
• Common Assembly
  • Bolt Action Assembly
  • Straight Pull Assembly
  • Dual Straight Pull Assembly

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• All variants share the common assembly steps first.
  • Use light pressure when seating bearings and avoid overtightening into printed threads.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Complete common assembly first, then apply bolt-action specific hardware and handle reinforcement.

docs.title: Step 1: Secure front pump bar anchors
docs.text:
• Insert a 6-32 hex nut into each front inner cutout.
  • Install a 6-32 Phillips screw from the outside on each side, passing through the pump bar.

docs.title: Step 2: Secure rear pump bar anchors
docs.text:
• Insert a 6-32 hex nut into each rear inner cutout.
  • Install a 6-32 Phillips screw from the outside on each side, passing through the pump bar.

docs.title: Step 3: Install top rear reinforcement
docs.text:
• Install one 6-32 socket head screw on each top rear side.
  • Seat fully without overtightening.

docs.title: Step 4: Reinforce handle screws
docs.text:
• Install two 6-32 2 inch Phillips screws into each handle.
  • Ensure screws are fully seated but not overtorqued.

docs.title: Step 5: Install handle heads
docs.text:
• Slide two HandleHead parts onto each handle with attachment hole facing down.
  • Secure each with a 6-32 socket head screw.

docs.title: Step 6: Install bottom bearing pair
docs.text:
• Install a 623 POM bearing on each bottom side.
  • Secure each with one 8 mm M3 socket head screw.

docs.title: Step 1: Install front bearing pair
docs.text:
• On the front side of the PrimeBlock, install two 623 POM bearings.
  • Secure with two 8 mm M3 socket head screws through the top.
  • Ensure each screw passes through the bearing center and does not pinch the race.

docs.title: Step 2: Install rear bearing pair
docs.text:
• On the rear side of the PrimeBlock, install two 623 POM bearings.
  • Secure with two 8 mm M3 socket head screws.
  • Inverted assembly orientation can make this placement easier.

docs.title: Step 3: Set barrel center support bearing
docs.text:
• Rotate PrimeBlock upside-down and pass the barrel through the center.
  • Place one 623 POM bearing in the center slot.
  • Tune bearing position for smooth barrel movement, then secure with one 8 mm M3 screw.

docs.title: Step 4: Install pump bars
docs.text:
• Align PumpBars at the rear of PrimeBlock with wider-spaced holes facing the block.
  • Slide bars until flush with the front and verify hole alignment.
  • Use gentle tapping only if required for fitment.

docs.title: Step 5: Install catch lever
docs.text:
• Position CatchLever in the bottom-rear cutout and align pivot hole.
  • Insert one 3/32 inch dowel through the side to retain the lever.
  • Ensure lever rotates freely after dowel is seated.

docs.title: Prime Block Dual Straight Pull Assembly
Overview
docs.text: Follow common assembly first, then install mirrored straight-pull reinforcement and hardware on both sides.

docs.title: Step 1: Complete common assembly
docs.text: • Finish all steps in Common Assembly.

docs.title: Step 2: Install mirrored front and rear anchors
docs.text: • Install 6-32 hex nuts and 6-32 Phillips screws at front and rear on both sides through pump bars.

docs.title: Step 3: Install reinforcement hardware
docs.text:
• Install top rear 6-32 socket head screws.
  • Install handle reinforcement screws and verify free movement.

docs.title: Step 4: Install handle heads and bottom bearings
docs.text:
• Install both handle heads and secure with 6-32 socket head screws.
  • Install bottom 623 bearings with 8 mm M3 socket head screws.

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• PrimeBlock
  • PumpBars
  • CatchLever
  • Barrel
  • 623 POM Bearing x7

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text:
• M3 Socket Head Screw, 8 mm x7
  • 3/32 inch Dowel x1

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text:
• 6-32 Hex Nut x4
  • 6-32 Phillips Screw x4
  • 6-32 Socket Head Screw x4
  • 6-32 Phillips Head Screw, 2 inch x4

docs.title: Components
docs.text: • HandleHead x2

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text:
• 6-32 Hex Nut x4
  • 6-32 Phillips Screw x4
  • 6-32 Socket Head Screw x4
  • 6-32 Phillips Head Screw, 2 inch x4

docs.title: Components
docs.text: • HandleHead x2

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text:
• 6-32 Hex Nut x4
  • 6-32 Phillips Screw x4
  • 6-32 Socket Head Screw x4
  • 6-32 Phillips Head Screw, 2 inch x4

docs.title: Components
docs.text: • HandleHead x2

docs.title: Common 1
docs.text: Install front 623 bearing pair with 8 mm M3 screws.

docs.title: Common 2
docs.text: Install rear 623 bearing pair with 8 mm M3 screws.

docs.title: Common 3
docs.text: Set center barrel support bearing and secure.

docs.title: Common 4
docs.text: Install PumpBars and align holes.

docs.title: Common 5
docs.text: Install CatchLever and 3/32 inch dowel.

docs.title: Variant
docs.text: Install front/rear anchor nuts and screws, reinforcement screws, handle heads, and bottom bearing pair per selected variant.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Follow the same fastening and reinforcement sequence as bolt action unless your printed variant has geometry-specific differences.

docs.title: Step 1: Complete common assembly
docs.text: • Finish all steps in Common Assembly.

docs.title: Step 2: Install front and rear anchor hardware
docs.text: • Install front and rear 6-32 hex nuts and 6-32 Phillips screws through pump bars on both sides.

docs.title: Step 3: Install reinforcement screws
docs.text:
• Install top rear 6-32 socket head screws, then handle reinforcement screws.
  • Keep torque moderate to avoid thread damage in printed parts.

docs.title: Step 4: Install handle heads and bottom bearings
docs.text:
• Install handle heads with 6-32 socket head screws.
  • Install bottom 623 bearings with 8 mm M3 socket head screws.

docs.title: Receiver Assembly
docs.text: View Materials List

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Assemble the trigger group into the receiver, install the grip and front locator dowel, and snap in the trigger guard.

docs.title: Step 1: Fit trigger and sear bushings
docs.text:
• Insert an 8 mm PTFE tube into the pivot hole of the Trigger.
  • Confirm a 3/32 inch dowel can pass through and rotate freely, then remove the dowel.
  • Repeat for the Sear.

docs.title: Step 2: Install trigger pivot
docs.text:
• Insert the Trigger into the bottom of the Receiver and align the holes.
  • Press a 3/32 inch dowel through the receiver and trigger until seated on both sides.
  • Verify the Trigger rotates freely.

docs.title: Step 3: Install sear and spring
docs.text:
• Move the Trigger forward and place a 0.625 inch compression spring in the receiver spring pocket.
  • Pull the Trigger back, position the Sear so teeth engage, and align pivot holes.
  • Press a 3/32 inch dowel through the sear pivot and release the Trigger to resting position.

docs.title: Step 4: Install grip
docs.text:
• Slide the Grip onto the back of the Receiver.
  • Secure with two 6-32 Phillips screws.

docs.title: Step 5: Install front locator dowel
docs.text:
• Install a 3/32 inch dowel in the top front slot of the Receiver.
  • Press fully into place.
  • If needed, lightly open the slot and use a small drop of superglue only if friction fit is insufficient.
  • ReceiverDualWindow variant: install the symmetric dowel on the opposite side.

docs.title: Step 6: Install trigger guard
docs.text:
• Hook the handle side of the TriggerGuard into the Receiver first.
  • Flex the front side in until it snaps into the front slot ahead of the Sear.

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• Receiver
  • Trigger
  • Sear
  • Grip
  • TriggerGuard

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text:
• 6-32 Phillips Screw x2
  • 3/32 inch Dowel x3
  • 0.625 inch Compression Spring x1

docs.title: Consumables
docs.text: • Superglue (optional)

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text: • 3/32 inch Dowel x1 (additional)

docs.title: Step 1
docs.text: Fit PTFE tube bushings in Trigger and Sear.

docs.title: Step 2
docs.text: Install Trigger and pivot dowel in Receiver.

docs.title: Step 3
docs.text: Install compression spring, then install Sear and pivot dowel.

docs.title: Step 4
docs.text: Install Grip with two 6-32 Phillips screws.

docs.title: Step 5
docs.text: Install front locator dowel (plus second dowel on ReceiverDualWindow).

docs.title: Step 6
docs.text: Snap TriggerGuard into place.

docs.title: Shroud Assembly
docs.text: View Materials List

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Assemble one of the front shroud variants for your BABP build. This section covers both the standard railgun shroud and the railgun bipod shroud path.

docs.title: Variant Paths
docs.text:
• Railgun Variant
  • Bipod Sub-Assembly
  • Bipod Variant

docs.title: Step 1: Choose a Variant
docs.text: Select either the railgun variant or the railgun bipod variant before starting hardware installation.

docs.title: Step 2: Complete the Matching Variant Page
docs.text: Follow the linked variant page in full before continuing to Receiver Assembly.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Assemble the locking bipod mechanism before inserting it into the bipod shroud body.

docs.title: Step 1: Install Right Leg
docs.text: Insert RightLeg into the right side of BipodCore and secure with 1x 6-32 socket head screw. The indent should face inward.

docs.title: Step 2: Install Left Leg
docs.text: Insert LeftLeg into the left side of BipodCore and secure with 1x 6-32 socket head screw. Confirm both leg indents face inward.

docs.title: Step 3: Prepare Lock Nubs and Spring
docs.text: Optionally smooth the front angled sections of RightLockNub and LeftLockNub. Place both lock nubs in BipodCap with nub faces inward, then insert the spring between them.

docs.title: Step 4: Install Cap
docs.text: Install the cap assembly onto the bottom of BipodCore and secure with 1x 6-32 Phillips screw. Confirm lock nubs move freely and spring back.

docs.title: Step 5: Prepare Button Dowels
docs.text: Optionally smooth the inner center edges of BipodButton. Insert 2x 3/32 inch dowels into the left and right holes of the button.

docs.title: Step 6: Install Button
docs.text: Insert the button into the front of the core, align top guides, and confirm pressing the button retracts both lock nubs.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This variant adds the integrated locking bipod and uses the bipod shroud body components.

docs.title: Step 1: Install Heat Set Insert
docs.text: Install 1x 6-32 heat set insert into RGB_Insert, flush to the part surface. Insert RGB_Insert into RGB_MainBody with the flat side up.

docs.title: Step 2: Insert Support Bars and Lock Insert
docs.text: Insert the longer RGB_TopSupportBar and shorter RGB_BottomSupportBar into the rear slots. Install 1x 6-32 Phillips screw into the bottom of RGB_MainBody to lock the insert and bottom support bar.

docs.title: Step 3: Attach Base
docs.text: Install RGB_Base to RGB_MainBody and secure with 5x 6-32 socket head screws. Do not overtighten.

docs.title: Step 4: Install Bipod Sub-Assembly
docs.text: Slide the completed Bipod Sub-Assembly into the front of RGB_MainBody. Use 1x 6-32 socket head screw through the button into the heat set insert. Keep it loose enough to allow swivel; Loctite is recommended.

docs.title: Step 5: Functional Check
docs.text: Confirm legs extend and lock, lock nubs engage in extended position, and bipod swivels side to side. Adjust screw tension if needed.

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text:
• 6-32 Socket Head Screw (see variant quantities)
  • 6-32 Phillips Screw (see variant quantities)

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• RG_MainBody
  • RG_Base
  • RG_TopSupportBar
  • RG_BottomSupportBar
  • RG_BottomRail (chosen length)

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text:
• 6-32 Phillips Screw x3
  • 6-32 Socket Head Screw x5

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• BipodCore
  • RightLeg
  • LeftLeg
  • RightLockNub
  • LeftLockNub
  • BipodCap
  • BipodButton

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text:
• 6-32 Socket Head Screw x2
  • 6-32 Phillips Screw x1
  • 3/32 inch Dowel x2
  • Bipod Lock Spring x1

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• RGB_MainBody
  • RGB_Base
  • RGB_Insert
  • RGB_TopSupportBar
  • RGB_BottomSupportBar
  • Bipod Sub-Assembly

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text:
• 6-32 Heat Set Insert x1
  • 6-32 Phillips Screw x1
  • 6-32 Socket Head Screw x6

docs.title: Consumables
docs.text: • Loctite (recommended)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This variant uses the RG shroud body and support bars without the integrated bipod assembly.

docs.title: Step 1: Install Bottom Rail
docs.text: Install your chosen length of RG_BottomRail to RG_MainBody with 3x 6-32 Phillips screws.

docs.title: Step 2: Insert Support Bars
docs.text: Insert the longer RG_TopSupportBar and shorter RG_BottomSupportBar into the rear slots of RG_MainBody.

docs.title: Step 3: Attach Base
docs.text: Install RG_Base to RG_MainBody and secure with 5x 6-32 socket head screws. Do not overtighten.

docs.title: Step 1: Choose Variant
docs.text: Use the standard railgun or railgun bipod variant path.

docs.title: Step 2: Build Shroud Variant
docs.text: Complete either Railgun Variant or Bipod Variant.

docs.title: Step 3: Continue To Receiver
docs.text: After shroud variant completion, proceed to Receiver Assembly.

docs.title: Stock/Magwell Assembly
docs.text: View Materials List

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Assembles the stock and magazine interface components.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text: This section will collect common failure modes, root-cause checks, and corrective actions.

docs.title: Initial Checks
docs.text:
• Confirm barrel is fully seated and locked.
  • Confirm core orientation is correct and catch engagement is positive.
  • Confirm o-rings are present, lubricated, and not damaged.
  • Confirm fasteners are tight but not overtorqued into printed threads.

docs.title: In Progress
docs.text: Detailed troubleshooting matrices will be expanded from the reference source.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text: This section covers seal tuning, friction reduction, periodic inspection, and service intervals.

docs.title: Initial Guidance
docs.text:
• Keep sliding interfaces clean and lightly lubricated.
  • Re-check barrel seal drag after major disassembly.
  • Inspect semi-consumables (for example catch bearings) regularly.
  • De-tension the blaster when stored long-term.

docs.title: In Progress
docs.text: Detailed tuning procedures will be expanded from the reference source.

docs.title: Turnaround Assembly
docs.text: View Materials List

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Turnaround redirects airflow from the plunger tube into the barrel and must be assembled with clean sealing surfaces.

docs.title: Step 1: Bond tube holder insert
docs.text:
• Apply a small amount of superglue to the angled face and top of TubeHolderInsert.
  • Insert TubeHolderInsert into the front side of TubeHolder.
  • Test-fit PlungerTube in the rear of TubeHolder, then remove the tube.

docs.title: Step 2: Install turnaround o-rings
docs.text:
• Install a 016 o-ring in the inside groove of Turnaround.
  • Install a 127 o-ring in the outer central groove of Turnaround.
  • Ensure both o-rings are fully seated and not twisted.

docs.title: Step 3: Seat plunger tube on turnaround
docs.text:
• Clean PlungerTube interior of debris.
  • Press PlungerTube onto the front of Turnaround over the 127 o-ring until fully seated.
  • Verify o-ring remains fully captured and not buckled.
  • Use a small amount of lubricant only if needed to help seating.

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• Turnaround
  • TubeHolder
  • TubeHolderInsert
  • PlungerTube

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text:
• 016 O-Ring x1
  • 127 O-Ring x1

docs.title: Consumables
docs.text:
• Superglue
  • Lubricant (optional)

docs.title: Step 1
docs.text: Glue TubeHolderInsert into TubeHolder and test-fit PlungerTube.

docs.title: Step 2
docs.text: Install 016 and 127 o-rings into Turnaround grooves.

docs.title: Step 3
docs.text: Clean and seat PlungerTube onto Turnaround, confirming o-ring capture.
